_meta.purpose: Flat, single-lookup view of the confidence/basis fields already nested inside tracks.json, generated for the ai teammate's ReferenceFact pattern so it can join on trackId in one step instead of walking the full track object. This is a derived convenience file, not a new source of truth — tracks.json remains authoritative; if the two ever disagree, tracks.json wins and this file is stale and needs regenerating.

albert_park.pitLossBasis: Mid-length pit lane, standard 80km/h-equivalent speed limit, no unusually long entry/exit.
albert_park.safetyCarBasis: Historically Albert Park had frequent early-race incidents at Turn 1/3 in its older layout; the 2022+ reprofiled layout has fewer walls and lower SC frequency than its reputation suggests, so 'medium' rather than 'high'.

shanghai.pitLossBasis: Long pit lane, known as one of the longer time-loss circuits.
shanghai.safetyCarBasis: Wide, permanent circuit with some barrier-heavy sections (Turn 14); moderate historical SC rate.

suzuka.pitLossBasis: Tight, tricky pit entry (130R approach) but standard-length pit lane.
suzuka.safetyCarBasis: High-speed, low-barrier-contact layout in the dry; weather (typhoon season) is the main SC/red-flag driver historically, not incidents.

bahrain.pitLossBasis: Long, wide pit straight; standard desert-circuit layout.
bahrain.safetyCarBasis: Wide run-off areas, few walls; historically one of the lower-SC-frequency circuits.

jeddah.pitLossBasis: Fast street circuit, high pit-lane entry/exit speed limits offset by wall-lined narrow lane.
jeddah.safetyCarBasis: Very high average speed, walls close to track edge in multiple sections; has had SC/red-flag incidents most years since 2021 debut.

miami.pitLossBasis: Relatively short, wide pit lane by modern standards.
miami.safetyCarBasis: Tight chicane sections (Turns 14-17) have produced incidents in most editions since 2022 debut.

imola.pitLossBasis: Widely cited as one of the highest pit-loss circuits on the calendar due to a long, low-speed-limit pit lane relative to a short, fast lap.
imola.safetyCarBasis: Narrow, wall-lined old-school layout (Villeneuve/Tosa/Rivazza) with limited run-off; regular SC history including notable 2020-2022 incidents.

monaco.pitLossBasis: Short pit lane in absolute terms, but the low speed limit and tight entry keep the loss non-trivial despite the short physical length.
monaco.safetyCarBasis: Tightest, most barrier-lined circuit on the calendar; historically among the most SC/red-flag-prone races.

barcelona.pitLossBasis: Standard permanent-circuit pit lane length and speed limit.
barcelona.safetyCarBasis: Wide run-off, low wall contact; historically one of the calendar's cleanest circuits for SC frequency.

montreal.pitLossBasis: Widely regarded as one of the shortest pit-loss circuits on the calendar — short pit lane, minimal detour from the racing line.
montreal.safetyCarBasis: Walls close to the track (notably the Wall of Champions at the final chicane); one of the calendar's most SC-prone circuits historically.

red_bull_ring.pitLossBasis: Short lap, standard pit lane; low-to-mid time loss.
red_bull_ring.safetyCarBasis: Short, high-density-of-incidents lap (multiple slow corners feeding straights) tends to produce a moderate SC rate despite generous run-off.

silverstone.pitLossBasis: Long, fast lap somewhat offsets a standard-length pit lane, giving a moderate relative loss.
silverstone.safetyCarBasis: High-speed corners (Copse/Maggotts-Becketts) raise incident severity when they happen, but generous run-off in most sections keeps frequency moderate rather than high.

spa.pitLossBasis: Long lap and a pit entry that allows relatively high speed keep the relative time loss on the lower end.
spa.safetyCarBasis: Longest circuit on the calendar with famously variable Ardennes weather; frequent weather-driven SC/red-flag periods.

hungaroring.pitLossBasis: Tight, twisty permanent circuit; standard mid-pack pit loss.
hungaroring.safetyCarBasis: Low average speed and generous run-off historically keep this one of the calendar's lower-SC circuits, despite being tight and overtaking-poor.

zandvoort.pitLossBasis: Narrow circuit with a tight, chicane-heavy layout increases relative pit-loss.
zandvoort.safetyCarBasis: Narrow track with limited overtaking and dune-side barriers in places; moderate SC history since its 2021 return.

monza.pitLossBasis: Very high average lap speed makes even a standard-length pit lane detour costly in relative terms.
monza.safetyCarBasis: Wide run-off at most chicanes/corners keeps incident rate low; mainly a low-SC circuit historically.

baku.pitLossBasis: Standard street-circuit pit lane, offset by a very long, fast back straight that lowers the relative cost of the lap portion.
baku.safetyCarBasis: Narrow street circuit with a notoriously tight castle-section chicane; one of the calendar's most SC/red-flag-prone venues most seasons.

singapore.pitLossBasis: Long, walled street-circuit pit lane; consistently one of the highest time-loss venues.
singapore.safetyCarBasis: Historically near-100% SC probability most years (only missed once, 2023, since the race joined the calendar).

cota.safetyCarBasis: Generous run-off in most sections; occasional Turn 1 first-lap incidents keep it from being 'low'.

mexico_city.pitLossBasis: Long pit lane through the stadium section area.
mexico_city.safetyCarBasis: Wide track surface, generous run-off in most corners; historically moderate-to-low SC rate.

interlagos.safetyCarBasis: Frequently wet/mixed-weather race weekend historically (Sao Paulo's rainy season overlaps the race date), driving up SC/VSC frequency independent of circuit layout risk.

las_vegas.pitLossBasis: Street circuit with very long, fast straights (Strip); standard street-circuit pit lane.
las_vegas.safetyCarBasis: Only debuted in 2023; sample size of 2-3 real races is too small for a confident historical tier yet.

lusail.pitLossBasis: Long, high-speed lap makes a standard pit lane detour relatively costly.
lusail.safetyCarBasis: High-speed, sweeping layout with some wall proximity; moderate historical SC rate.

yas_marina.safetyCarBasis: Sourced figure: ~38% Safety Car probability and ~38% VSC probability historically.

madring.pitLossBasis: New venue, no real-world race data yet (first race Sept 2026); no basis for even an informed estimate beyond 'hybrid circuit similar to Miami' as a rough analogue.
madring.safetyCarBasis: No historical races run yet as of 2026-07-09; first race scheduled Sept 11-13 2026.
